title: Intention to create legal relations
summary: 9084 Contract — domestic/social vs commercial presumptions and rebuttal.
learningObjectives: Explain presumptions in domestic and commercial agreements.
Analyse cases: Balfour v Balfour, Merritt v Merritt, Esso v Commissioners.
Evaluate how intention is proved or disproved.
Apply to social, family, and business contexts.

simpleExplanation.title: Did they mean to be bound?
simpleExplanation.summary: Commercial agreements presume legal intent; domestic arrangements usually don't — unless evidence shows otherwise.

flashcards.back: Strong presumption that parties **intend legal relations** — rarely rebutted (Esso v Commissioners).
Presumption **against** legal intent — family arrangements not normally contracts (Balfour v Balfour).

flashcards.front: How was Balfour v Balfour rebutted in Merritt?
flashcards.back: Separated spouses, written agreement, clear terms — court found **intention to be bound** (*Merritt v Merritt*).
Trade union/workplace deals — presumption against legal enforcement unless clear intent (Ford Motor Co v AUEFW).
Written formal agreement, separation, business-like conduct, explicit promise to pay.
All four formation elements required — lack of intention means no contract even if agreement + consideration exist.

sections.question: Mr Balfour, working in Ceylon, agreed to pay his wife £30/month while she remained in England. They later separated and she sued for arrears. Advise on intention.
sections.solution:
**Presumption:** Domestic agreement between **husband and wife living together** — **presumption against** legal intention (*Balfour v Balfour*).

**Facts:** Agreement made during **cohabitation**; informal arrangement about maintenance while apart for work — typical **domestic** context.

**Rebuttal?** No written formal contract; no separation at time of agreement. Unlike *Merritt v Merritt*, parties were **not separated** when promise made.

**Conclusion:** **No contract** — Mrs Balfour cannot sue. Agreement was a **domestic arrangement**, not intended to create legal relations.

**Contrast:** If they had **separated** and signed a written agreement to pay £30/month, *Merritt* would apply — intention likely found.
